behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is key-derivation logic at a single function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because the behavior text is only about deterministic key generation from parameters, with no module handoff requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit test for `generateCacheKey(params)`: assert same normalized params produce identical keys across repeated calls; assert distinct param sets (including ordering/casing edge cases defined by spec) produce distinct keys.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is deterministic mapping from category/config to TTL. REAL-INFRA: none required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text targets computation correctness, not cross-module propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test for `computeTTL(category, freshnessConfig)`: verify exact TTL outputs for each configured category, default/fallback behavior, and boundary values (missing/invalid category).

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure typically appears at module boundary between memory-usage provider, config, and strategy selector. Unit alone can miss wiring/state propagation bugs. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process fakes (no OS/network/subprocess required), so not System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring cache manager + memory monitor abstraction + config: drive usage below and above threshold and assert active strategy transitions LRU->LFU according to config, including hysteresis/reset behavior if implemented.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on a real PostgreSQL write path (subprocess/networked DB semantics, transaction/notification timing). MOCK-HIDE: mocked DB/event emitters can pass while real Postgres trigger/listen/commit-order behavior fails. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because the behavior text says "write to the underlying PostgreSQL table," which requires validating real DB interaction boundary.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real PostgreSQL instance and real cache layer: seed cache entries, execute actual table write/update/delete in Postgres, then assert all and only matching cache keys are invalidated after commit.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is policy algorithm correctness within eviction component. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on policy correctness, not inter-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests for eviction policy objects: construct deterministic access patterns, fill cache to capacity, then assert exact victim key under LRU and LFU modes (including tie-break rules if specified).
